excluded_defs.name: invalidCacheKey
excluded_defs.reason: read: explains why bindings.asKey() returns null when bindings contain ResolvedRecursiveType (IdentityEqualityType check). Investigated to confirm that asKey() will still return null for partially-resolved bindings, so the else-if branch correctly falls through when resolution is incomplete.

excluded_defs.name: asKey
excluded_defs.reason: read: confirms asKey() returns null for bindings containing IdentityEqualityType (ResolvedRecursiveType), establishing the root cause of the missing cache entry

exploration_log.aha_moment: TypeBindings.invalidCacheKey() returns true when the bindings contain any IdentityEqualityType (including ResolvedRecursiveType), making asKey() return null, so key is null from the start and the normal caching path `if (key != null...)` is bypassed entirely. The fix adds an else-if branch that detects this case via context.hasSelfReferences() and retries asKey() after resolution.

dead_ends.explored: Whether modifying TypeBindings.invalidCacheKey() to skip resolved ResolvedRecursiveType would be cleaner
dead_ends.why_irrelevant: Would change TypeBindings semantics for all callers; the TypeFactory-local else-if branch is more surgical and targeted

exploration_log.hindsight: Should have checked TypeBindings.invalidCacheKey() and asKey() first to understand the null-key root cause rather than tracing through the caching condition in detail.

alternatives: Modify TypeBindings.invalidCacheKey() to treat resolved ResolvedRecursiveType as valid
Store result indexed by rawType without bindings
key_decisions.decision: Add else-if branch in _fromClass() that retries asKey() after resolution and caches if a valid key can be produced
key_decisions.reasoning: Keeps the fix contained to _fromClass() and uses the existing asKey() contract; doesn't change TypeBindings semantics for other callers

queries.query_text: Fix TypeFactory._fromClass() to cache fully-resolved self-referential types after resolveSelfReferences() using ClassStack.hasSelfReferences() to verify no unresolved ResolvedRecursiveType remain

solve_notes: The root cause: when type bindings contain a ResolvedRecursiveType placeholder, TypeBindings.asKey() returns null (because invalidCacheKey() fires for IdentityEqualityType instances), so the normal cache path is bypassed. After resolveSelfReferences() patches the placeholder, a post-resolution key can be attempted. Added hasSelfReferences() to ClassStack to distinguish self-referential frames from plain recursive-call frames, and added an else-if branch in _fromClass() to retry caching with the post-resolution key.
task_text:
`TypeFactory` uses `ResolvedRecursiveType` as a placeholder when it
detects a type that references itself (e.g., `Comparable<Foo>` where
`Foo implements Comparable<Foo>`). After resolution, `resolveSelfReferences()`
patches the placeholder, but the code comment at the caching step
acknowledges that types containing a partially-resolved
`ResolvedRecursiveType` are skipped from the cache. This causes repeated
expensive resolution on every access. Fix the caching logic in
`TypeFactory._fromClass()` to correctly cache fully-resolved
self-referential types once `resolveSelfReferences()` has completed,
verifying that no unresolved `ResolvedRecursiveType` references remain.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: resolveSelfReferences
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: shows exactly how self-references are patched; confirms that after this call all ResolvedRecursiveType instances in _selfRefs have _referencedType set, making the type cacheable

thrash_preventing_defs.name: addSelfReference
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: shows when and how ResolvedRecursiveType instances are registered; confirms that hasSelfReferences() correctly identifies self-referential context frames

thrash_preventing_defs.name: invalidCacheKey
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: explains WHY key is null during self-referential type construction (IdentityEqualityType check); needed to understand why the normal caching path fires key==null for these types

thrash_preventing_defs.name: getSelfReferencedType
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: confirms that _referencedType is null before resolution and non-null after; validates the post-resolution caching approach

tier_difference_reasoning: minimum_sufficient covers the two edited sites (_fromClass and the new hasSelfReferences method). thrash_preventing adds ClassStack.resolveSelfReferences and addSelfReference (an agent needs to read both to understand when placeholders are registered vs. resolved), TypeBindings.invalidCacheKey (explains the root cause of null key without which an agent would not know why asKey() returns null), and ResolvedRecursiveType.getSelfReferencedType (confirms the post-resolution state). Without these, an agent would likely make wrong assumptions about when the placeholder is considered resolved or why the null-key branch exists.
